text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint little village called Willowbrook, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He had feathers of soft grey with brown stripes. Every evening, he would watch the sun dip below the willows. One unusually gloomy afternoon, Oliver decided he'd spy something interesting.

He perched near the old oak tree and listened. Birds chirped merrily. He hooted softly. Suddenly, a tiny bird fluttered past. Oliver followed. It landed beside a watering bowl for foxes. The fox studied it. Maybe lunch. Oliver whispered.

The bird fluttered skyward. Perhaps it fledsouthward?

Night fell. Oliver flew southward too. After a weary night, he woke. Somehow, he found berries! Willowbrook was grander.

Every day he found something new. He even befriended a squirrel. They discussed forests.

One day, Oliver saw a flock flying fast. They must migrate!

Off he went. Spring thaw came
